item.title: The Advent of Fare-Gate Society
item.description: Some cities are realizing that the only way to provide high-quality public services is through designs that keep disorder out.
item.dc:creator: Henry Grabar

item.title: DOJ’s First ‘Weaponization’ Report Is a Bust
item.description: The document purports to show bias under the Biden administration—and fails spectacularly.
item.dc:creator: Quinta Jurecic

item.title: The Most Tortured Relationship in America
item.description: Monogamy is one of the last bipartisan ideals—even if people struggle to live up to it.
item.dc:creator: Faith Hill

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Free Bread
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: Where Did ‘Let Them’ Come From?
item.description: Years before Mel Robbins published her best-selling self-help book, a struggling writer posted a poem with a similar message.
item.dc:creator: Olga Khazan

item.title: What I Learned About Billionaires at Jeff Bezos’s Private Retreat
item.description: For the richest men on Earth, everything is free and nothing matters.
item.dc:creator: Noah Hawley

item.title: Catch
item.dc:creator: John Waters

item.title: Photos: Out Among the Cherry Blossoms
item.description: Recent images of people enjoying themselves on warm spring days, among groves of flowering cherry-blossom trees in cities and parks across the Northern Hemisphere
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: A 13,000-Mile Mission for One Beautiful Loaf
item.description: A conversation with Caity Weaver about a completely scientific, totally exhaustive search for America’s best free bread.
item.dc:creator: Rafaela Jinich

item.title: The Problem With Hasan Piker’s Einstein Story
item.description: People scrutinizing influencers for their views should also hold them to account for their facts.
item.dc:creator: Yair Rosenberg

item.title: California’s Blue Armageddon
item.description: One of the most liberal states in the country can’t find a Democrat to lead it.
item.dc:creator: Mark Leibovich

item.title: Scars
item.dc:creator: Sigrid Nunez

item.title: The President and the Pope
item.description: Panelists joined to discuss Trump’s attacks on Pope Leo XIV for his comments about the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: The Editors

item.title: How to Revive the Art of Hanging Out
item.description: Modern life makes it harder to seek out places to just be, but it’s not impossible.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Fattal

item.title: A Beguiling Film About the Downsides of Pop Stardom
item.description: Mother Maryoffers a spooky spin on what it takes to stay famous.
item.dc:creator: David Sims

item.title: Is Hurry the Great Enemy of Spiritual Life?
item.description: Pastor John Mark Comer has won a massive audience by encouraging his followers to free themselves from the gnawing sense that there is always more to do.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Walecki

item.title: The Film That Explains Contemporary America
item.description: The Sorrow and the Pityhas lessons for how authoritarianism takes root—and how to fight against it.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: Ukraine Has Finally Given Up on Trump
item.description: Zelensky has written off the United States.
item.dc:creator: Phillips Payson O’Brien

item.title: The FBI Director Is MIA
item.description: Kash Patel has alarmed colleagues with episodes of excessive drinking and unexplained absences.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fitzpatrick

item.title: The Donald J. Trump Guide to Classic Fairy Tales
item.description: Has the president failed to learn the lessons of classic cautionary fables—or does he just understand them in his own novel ways?
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham
